Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student enrolled in Top Grade Tutorials, including personal and academic details. This entity stores information necessary for student management, attendance, and accounting.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

name.description: The full name of the student.

classGrade.description: The current grade or class level of the student (e.g., '6th', '7th'). (Dropdown options: 6th to 10th grade)

rollNumber.description: The unique roll number assigned to the student, used for identification, search, and attendance tracking.

fatherName.description: The full name of the student's father or primary guardian.

mobileNumber.description: The mobile contact number for the student or their guardian.

monthlyFee.description: The agreed monthly tuition fee for the student.

imageUrl.description: URL to the student's profile image.

dateAdded.description: The date and time when the student record was initially added to the system.

Teacher.title: Teacher
Teacher.description: Represents a teacher in Top Grade Tutorials, including their contact information. Teachers can also be associated with activities like collecting payments and generating question papers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Teacher entity.

name.description: The full name of the teacher.

mobileNumber.description: The mobile contact number of the teacher.

imageUrl.description: URL to the teacher's profile image.

dateAdded.description: The date and time when the teacher record was initially added to the system.

Payment.title: Payment
Payment.description: Records a payment made by a student for their monthly fees, detailing the amount, date of payment, and the teacher who collected it. This entity supports accounting and receipt generation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Payment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student who made the payment. (Relationship: Student 1:N Payment)

teacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher who collected the payment. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Payment)

amount.description: The amount of money paid in this transaction.

paymentDate.description: The date and time when the payment was recorded.

paymentMonth.description: The month and year for which the fee is being paid (e.g., 'YYYY-MM').

receiptNumber.description: A unique identifier for the payment receipt, allowing for easy lookup and printing.

QuestionPaper.title: QuestionPaper
QuestionPaper.description: Stores details about a dynamically generated question paper by the AI tool, including its parameters and a link to the generated document for download.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QuestionPaper entity.

classGrade.description: The class grade for which the question paper is generated.

subject.description: The subject of the question paper (e.g., 'Math', 'Science').

chapter.description: The specific chapter(s) or topics covered by the question paper.

questionType.description: The type of questions included in the paper (e.g., 'Creative', 'MCQ').

numberOfQuestions.description: The total number of questions in the generated paper.

timeLimitMinutes.description: The time limit in minutes allocated for students to complete the exam.

totalMarks.description: The total marks for the question paper.

generatedPdfUrl.description: URL to the downloadable PDF document of the generated question paper.

generationDate.description: The date and time when the question paper was generated.

generatedByTeacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher who initiated the generation of this question paper. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N QuestionPaper)

InstitutionSettings.title: InstitutionSettings
InstitutionSettings.description: Stores global settings and customizable information for the Top Grade Tutorials institution, such as its name and logo. This is expected to be a singleton entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InstitutionSettings entity. (Expected to be a fixed ID like 'defaultSettings' as it's a singleton).

institutionName.description: The official name of the institution (e.g., 'Top Grade Tutorials').

logoUrl.description: URL to the institution's official logo image.

lastUpdated.description: The date and time when the institution settings were last updated.

definition.description: Contains records of all enrolled students. Access is restricted to authenticated teachers and administrators. No explicit denormalization for authorization is needed as access is role-based for the entire collection.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student.

definition.description: Contains records of all registered teachers. Access is restricted to authenticated administrators for management, and teachers can read their own profiles. No explicit denormalization for authorization is needed as access is role-based.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The unique identifier for the teacher.

definition.description: Records all student fee payments. Includes `studentId` and `teacherId` for direct authorization checks and querying. This enables Authorization Independence and QAPs by allowing rules to check `request.resource.data.teacherId == request.auth.uid` for teachers creating payments, or filtering lists by `teacherId`.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the payment transaction.

definition.description: Stores details of AI-generated question papers. Includes `generatedByTeacherId` for direct authorization checks and querying. This enables Authorization Independence and QAPs by allowing rules to check `request.resource.data.generatedByTeacherId == request.auth.uid` for teachers creating papers, or filtering lists by `generatedByTeacherId`.

params.name: paperId
params.description: The unique identifier for the question paper.

definition.description: Stores global institution settings, expected to be a singleton document (e.g., with ID 'defaultSettings'). Only administrators have write access.

params.name: settingsId
params.description: The unique identifier for the institution settings document (expected to be a fixed ID like 'defaultSettings').

definition.description: A collection where the mere existence of a document with `adminUid` as its ID grants administrative privileges to that user. The document content can be minimal (e.g., `{ active: true }`). Used for DBAC. Due to schema constraints, 'Teacher' is used as the entity reference, though it represents a generic admin role.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The UID of the authenticated user who has administrative privileges.

definition.description: A collection where the mere existence of a document with `teacherUid` as its ID grants teacher privileges to that user. The document content can be minimal (e.g., `{ active: true }`). Used for DBAC, allowing teachers to manage students, collect payments, and generate question papers. 'Teacher' is the appropriate entity reference.

params.name: teacherUid
params.description: The UID of the authenticated user who has teacher privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for 'Top Grade Academy Manager' is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control) to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design prioritizes authorization independence by denormalizing ownership and role information directly into the documents or using dedicated role collections. For instance, `Payment` documents include `teacherId` (the ID of the teacher who collected the payment) and `QuestionPaper` documents include `generatedByTeacherId`. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent documents or external collections for authorization context, making rules atomic, efficient, and easier to debug. For global roles, dedicated collections like `/roles_admin/{adminUid}` and `/roles_teacher/{teacherUid}` are used, where the mere existence of a document grants the role, further ensuring independence from other entity structures.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
Query-Ability and Predictability (QAPs) are ensured through structural segregation and the inclusion of denormalized authorization fields. For `/students` and `/teachers` collections, `list` operations are secured by checking the `request.auth.uid` against the `/roles_admin` and `/roles_teacher` collections. For `/payments` and `/question_papers`, teachers can perform `list` queries that filter by their own `teacherId` or `generatedByTeacherId` respectively (e.g., `db.collection('payments').where('teacherId', '==', currentUser.uid)`). The security rules will then validate that the `request.query.where.teacherId` matches `request.auth.uid` for teacher-level access, while admins retain full `list` access. This structure prevents unauthorized data exposure during `list` operations and ensures efficient, index-backed queries.

**Structural Segregation:**
Each top-level collection (`students`, `teachers`, `payments`, `question_papers`, `institution_settings`, `roles_admin`, `roles_teacher`) represents a distinct entity or security posture. This ensures that all documents within a given collection share homogeneous security requirements, simplifying Firestore security rules significantly. For example, all `Student` documents are managed by institution staff, not by students themselves.

**Access Modeling & DBAC:**

*   **Private Data & Hierarchical Paths:** User roles are implemented using `Path-Based Ownership` via dedicated collections `/roles_admin/{adminUid}` and `/roles_teacher/{teacherUid}`, where the document ID `adminUid` or `teacherUid` directly corresponds to the authenticated user's UID. This provides a clear, scalable, and auditable DBAC system.
*   **Centralized Institution Data:** Entities like `students`, `teachers`, `payments`, `question_papers`, and `institution_settings` are managed centrally by users with appropriate roles (admin, teacher) rather than individual user ownership, reflecting the institutional nature of the application. `InstitutionSettings` is designed as a singleton collection with a single document (e.g., ID 'defaultSettings') for global configuration.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
Semantic naming conventions are strictly applied (e.g., `{studentId}`, `{teacherId}`). Required fields for authorization (`teacherId`, `generatedByTeacherId`) are explicitly included in the respective documents, making their purpose clear. This design enables robust security rules, efficient querying, and simplified data management for the 'Top Grade Tutorials' application.
